GitHub

LambdaLLG.jl

LambdaLLG.jl integrates Landau-Lifshitz-Gilbert (LLG) spin dynamics in 1D and 2D, including local Gilbert damping and optional nonlocal damping kernels.

Features

  • 1D and 2D lattice LLG evolution with open boundaries.
  • Local and nonlocal damping terms.
  • Optional staggered (two-sublattice) fields and damping kernels.
  • DifferentialEquations.jl integration with per-step spin normalization.

Installation

For local development:

] activate .
] develop /path/to/LambdaLLG.jl

Then:

using LambdaLLG

Quick Start (1D)

using LambdaLLG
using Random

Random.seed!(4)
Nx = 32

p = LLGParams1D(
    Nx,
    1.0,
    (0.0, 0.0, -0.02),
    (0.0, 0.0, -0.1),
    0.05,
)

s0 = zeros(3, Nx)
for i in 1:Nx
    theta = 0.25
    phi = 2pi * rand()
    s0[:, i] .= (sin(theta) * cos(phi), sin(theta) * sin(phi), cos(theta))
end

sol = evolve1D(s0, (0.0, 40.0), p)
data = format_results(sol)

data[:, 1] contains time. The remaining columns store flattened spin components at each saved time step.

Output Shape

  • 1D state vectors are stored as length 3*Nx.
  • 2D state vectors are stored as length 3*Nx*Ny.
  • format_results(sol) returns a matrix with shape (nt, 1 + nstate).

Next Steps

  • Use examples.md for notebook-oriented workflows.
  • Use api.md for exported API references.
